daily_brief: Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran dominated headlines as both nations exchanged strikes overnight, with Tehran targeting US military bases in Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain while Washington conducted waves of attacks on Iranian coastal defenses and missile sites [BBC World], [France 24], [Globe and Mail]. The confrontation, which followed Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, has pushed Brent crude to a one-month high and raised fears of broader regional conflict. In Europe, France's National Assembly passed landmark assisted dying legislation after years of debate, though the law still faces Constitutional Council review [RFI], [BBC Europe]. A major surveillance investigation revealed that Morocco extensively deployed Pegasus spyware against journalists, politicians, and activists across multiple countries, based on testimony from an intelligence whistleblower [The Guardian], [Le Monde]. Argentina's dramatic 2-1 World Cup semifinal victory over England was overshadowed by players displaying a politically charged Falkland Islands banner, prompting FIFA disciplinary scrutiny [BBC World], [Al Jazeera], [France 24]. Meanwhile, TSMC announced an additional $100 billion US investment in Arizona chipmaking facilities [Nikkei Asia], and protests erupted across Ukraine following the surprise removal of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov [France 24].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Exchange Fresh Strikes as Hormuz Crisis Deepens
bullets: Iran launched drone attacks on US military bases in Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain, targeting radar systems, Patriot air defense installations, and fuel storage facilities
US Central Command conducted two waves of strikes on Iranian command centers, air defense sites, and coastal surveillance facilities including targets in Bandar Abbas and Greater Tunb Island
Iran warned it would target regional infrastructure if the US attacks Iranian infrastructure, calling the Strait of Hormuz a 'red line'
Brent crude closed at a one-month high of $84.95 per barrel as the strait, which carried about a fifth of global oil and gas shipments, remains blocked

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on military actions and Trump's warning to Iran to 'behave'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iranian threats and regional infrastructure warnings

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iran's 'existential war' rhetoric and economic implications

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Featured former US ambassador analysis suggesting Iran miscalculated Trump's resolve

top_stories.headline: France Passes Assisted Dying Law After Years of Parliamentary Deadlock
bullets: French National Assembly voted 291-241 to approve legislation granting terminally ill adults the right to end their lives with medical assistance
Law applies to French adults with 'serious and incurable' life-threatening illness in advanced or terminal stage causing unbearable suffering resistant to treatment
Patients must self-administer lethal substance after 15-day consultation period and two-day reflection period; medical professionals may assist if patient is unable
Prime Minister Lecornu will refer the text to Constitutional Council for review before it can become law

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed legislative process and constitutional referral concerns

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasized strict conditions and placed in context of UK debate on similar legislation

top_stories.headline: Whistleblower Reveals Morocco's Extensive Use of Pegasus Spyware
bullets: Former member of Morocco's domestic intelligence service (DGST) provided testimony that Morocco began using Pegasus spyware in 2017 and deployed it for four years
Targets allegedly included journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians, and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers
Morocco has long denied any relationship with NSO Group, the Israeli manufacturer of Pegasus
Revelations form basis of multiyear investigation marking five years since original Pegasus Project disclosures

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Led with whistleblower testimony and detailed targeting of European officials

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Multiple articles examining Morocco's surveillance tool purchases and ongoing judicial investigations

top_stories.headline: Argentina Reaches World Cup Final with Late Comeback Against England; Falklands Banner Sparks Diplomatic Tension
bullets: Argentina defeated England 2-1 with two goals in final minutes after trailing from the 55th minute, setting up Sunday's final against Spain
After the match, Argentine players displayed banner reading 'Las Malvinas son Argentinas' (The Falklands are Argentine)
FIFA previously fined Argentina's Football Association £20,000 in 2014 for similar political display; new disciplinary action expected
Argentine Vice President Victoria Villarruel posted video appearing to show Argentine soldiers, writing 'it wasn't just another match'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed FIFA rules and 1982 conflict history

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted sovereignty dispute and FIFA's ban on political displays

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on match details and upcoming final against Spain

top_stories.headline: TSMC Announces Additional $100 Billion US Investment in Arizona Chip Facilities
bullets: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. will invest additional $100 billion to build cutting-edge chipmaking facilities and advanced packaging plants in Arizona
Investment driven by AI demand; company raised full-year capital expenditure to $64 billion
Revenue growth forecast increased to 40% for the year
Expansion follows earlier TSMC commitments to US manufacturing amid geopolitical tensions over Taiwan

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized AI demand driver and connection to broader US investment strategy

additional_stories.headline: Over 500 feared dead in two suspected shipwrecks off Myanmar coast, UN warns
Rare protests erupt across Ukraine demanding reinstatement of removed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov
Fire at orphanage outside Algiers kills at least 11 amid Algerian heatwave
EU and Ukraine sign drone partnership deal to combine European industrial capacity with Ukrainian battlefield expertise
US Defense Secretary Hegseth orders testosterone deficiency screening for military personnel over 30
Second American Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment as DRC outbreak continues
Canadian wildfire smoke pushes Toronto air quality to world's worst, drifts into northeastern US ahead of World Cup final
Italy's Meloni coalition loses surprise secret ballot vote on electoral reform amendment
Families of 43 victims await first verdict in Genoa bridge collapse trial nearly eight years after disaster
New York Times moves to quash subpoenas served to journalists over Air Force One security reporting
